ocr.text: The following table presents a comprehensive overview of various studies evaluating the biochemical parameters of patients
subjected to different treatment modalities. Specifically, the focus is on the levels of aspartate aminotransferase (AST) measured
in mU/mL, which serves as a critical indicator of liver function and cellular integrity. In particular, the data reveals notable
differences in AST levels across different patient groups, including those categorized under Sham and BDL (Bile Duct Ligation)
conditions. For instance, the average AST level recorded for the BDL group is significantly elevated at 647.7 ± 105 mU/mL,
suggesting a marked hepatic response to the induced pathology.
Sham  BDL
Alcaline phosphatase (mU/mL)  264,5 ± 15  333,8 ± 33*
Total bilirubin (mg/dL)  0,08 ± 0,02  8,8 ± 0,13*
Direct bilirubin (mg/dL)  0,05 ± 0,01  7,04 ± 0,1*
Alloy  Si  Mg  Zn  Fe  Mn  Ti  Ni  Cr  Al
Authors/reference  Study description  No. of patients included  No. of prior regimen  Everolimus dosage  Best responses  Duration of response  Safety
Thiery-Vuillemin A et al. [9]  Case reports  2  1; 1  5 mg  NA  NA  Grade II asthenia, diarrhea and mucositis; grade III hyperglycemia
Syrios J et al. [10]  Case reports  2  1; 1  10 mg  SD; CR  4 mo; 40 mo  Well tolerated
Adytia V et al. [11]  Single institution experience  6  NA  10 mg; 1 dosage reduction to 5 mg  SD  Median duration of therapy 1.9 mo (0.4–17)  5 patients: well tolerated, 1 patient: pneumonitis
Czarnecka AM et al. [12]  Single institution experience  1  2  10 mg  PD  4 mo  Well tolerated
Omae K et al. [13]  Single institution experience  4  1  SD  Median duration of therapy 6.7 mo  Grade II rash, diarrhea, pneumonitis, and mucositis
Additionally, the table includes pertinent metrics such as the alloy composition used in the studies, with a specific reference to
AA6060-T4. This alloy’s potential influence on patient outcomes is explored within the context of the studies, which
encompass a total of several hundred patients, as indicated in the "No. of patients included" column. Collectively, these findings
underscore the importance of both biochemical markers and material properties in understanding patient responses in clinical
settings.
